title: rubia cordifolia subsp. pratensis
intro: Rubia cordifolia subsp. pratensis, also known as Indian madder or woodland madder, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its roots containing anthraquinones that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Rubia cordifolia subsp. pratensis are believed to provide various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or powder, and can be used to make herbal remedies that promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
